mission.title: Install and Configure Valkey
mission.description: Valkey is a high-performance distributed key-value database optimized for caching and real-time workloads. It is suitable for applications requiring fast data access and flexible data structures. Key trade-offs include the need for careful resource management and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Valkey Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Valkey Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add valkey https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3

```

> This command adds the Valkey repository for version 9.0.3.

steps.title: Install Valkey CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Valkey to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3/valkey-crds.yaml

```

> Ensure that the CRDs are installed before deploying the application.

steps.title: Install Valkey using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Valkey in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --version 9.0.3

```

> Replace 'valkey' with your desired release name.

steps.title: Create an example Valkey Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to configure Valkey.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: example-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a Valkey instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: example-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Valkey is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Valkey deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> You should see the Valkey pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Valkey
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Valkey deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall valkey --namespace valkey

kubectl delete crd valkeys.valkey.io

```

> This command will remove the Valkey release.
> This command will delete the Valkey CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Valkey
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up your data, upgrading the installation, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get valkeys.valkey.io example-valkey -o yaml > backup-example-valkey.yaml

helm upgrade valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --version 9.0.3

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> Backup the current configuration.
> Upgrade to the new version.
> Verify that the Valkey pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get valkeys.valkey.io example-valkey -o yaml > backup-example-valkey.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Valkey server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Valkey server may not be running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Valkey pods with 'kubectl get pods -n valkey' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Valkey CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition for Valkey may not be installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3/valkey-crds.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in the Valkey deployment and adjust your cluster resources accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC settings may not allow the current user to access Valkey resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the user has the appropriate permissions set in the RBAC configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new caching algorithms and improved data structure support.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older TLS versions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from version 8.x, ensure to update your configuration files to match the new schema.

resolution.summary: You should see the Valkey pods in a Running state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n valkey'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: example-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
